compositional_truth_check.findings: The registry provided does not match the visual evidence. The pots contain no visible foliage, only soil and debris. The 'White Rabbit' anchor is present in the lower pot, but the plants themselves are absent or entirely desiccated/submerged in substrate.

plant_audit.p1_yellow_pot: Empty/Soil only. No biological activity detected.
plant_audit.p2_black_pot: Empty/Soil only. No biological activity detected.
plant_audit.p3_black_pot_with_rabbit: Contains white rabbit figurine; no plant matter visible.
plant_audit.p4_shared_black_pot: Empty/Soil only. No biological activity detected.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Appears dark, potentially over-saturated or lacking organic life.
biome_observations.surface_debris: Presence of white particulate matter (possibly perlite or mineral salts) and a spoon in the yellow pot.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_current: The sequence shows a static environment. There is no growth, no movement, and no change in the state of the soil or the position of the anchor over the 5-day period.

visual_health_inference.conclusion: The biome is currently non-viable. All registered specimens are either dead, removed, or buried beneath the soil surface. The environment is currently a 'dead' system.

anomalies: Presence of a metal spoon in the yellow pot (P1).
Total absence of expected botanical specimens.
White rabbit anchor is the only non-soil object in P3.
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a multi-stage audit of the provided image sequence. My first action was to map the physical coordinates of the pots against the provided registry. Upon validation, I confirmed that the registry is entirely disconnected from the visual reality of the desk. The pots are devoid of any living plant matter. The 'White Rabbit' anchor is present, but the biological components (Pothos, Mint, etc.) are entirely absent. The system is in a state of total botanical failure.
